name: matrix_data_processing
description: Data wrangling: CSV cleaning, cross-format joins, and format discovery.

episodes.description: Clean a messy CSV with mixed date formats, duplicates, and inconsistent currency.

steps.name: clean_csv
steps.input:
Here is a messy CSV dataset:

id,date,product,revenue,region
1,2024-01-15,Widget A,"$1,234.56",East
2,01/20/2024,Widget B,567.89,West
3,Jan 25 2024,Widget A,$890.12,East
4,2024-02-01,Widget C,"$2,345.67",North
5,02/10/2024,Widget B,$456.78,West
6,Feb 15 2024,Widget A,1234.56,East
7,2024-02-20,Widget C,$789.01,North
1,2024-01-15,Widget A,"$1,234.56",East
8,03/01/2024,Widget B,"$1,567.89",West
9,Mar 5 2024,Widget A,$234.56,East
10,2024-03-10,Widget C,890.12,North
11,2024-03-15,Widget B,,West
12,03/20/2024,Widget A,$567.89,East
5,02/10/2024,Widget B,$456.78,West
13,2024-03-25,Widget C,"$1,123.45",North

Clean this data: (1) Normalize ALL dates to YYYY-MM-DD, (2) Remove duplicate rows (same id), (3) Strip $ signs and commas from revenue — plain numbers only, (4) Replace missing revenue with 0.

Reply as strict JSON: {"row_count": <after dedup>, "cleaned_csv": "<full CSV with header>", "issues_found": ["<issue1>", ...]}. No markdown, just JSON.

episodes.description: Join data from JSON, CSV, and XML sources into a unified structure.

steps.name: join_data
steps.input:
Join these three data sources into a unified dataset.

users.json:
[{"user_id":1,"name":"Alice Chen","email":"alice@example.com"},{"user_id":2,"name":"Bob Smith","email":"bob@corp.io"},{"user_id":3,"name":"Carol Diaz","email":"carol@example.com"}]

orders.csv:
order_id,user_id,product_id,amount
101,1,P001,29.99
102,1,P002,49.99
103,2,P001,29.99
104,3,P003,15.50
105,2,P003,15.50

products.xml:
<products><product id="P001"><name>Wireless Mouse</name><category>Electronics</category></product><product id="P002"><name>Desk Lamp</name><category>Home Office</category></product><product id="P003"><name>Notebook</name><category>Stationery</category></product></products>

For each user, produce a summary with their orders including product details. Reply as a strict JSON array. Each element: {"user_name": ..., "email": ..., "orders": [{"product_name": ..., "category": ..., "amount": ...}]}. Include all 3 users. No markdown, just JSON.

checks.rubric: Valid JSON array with 3 elements. Alice has 2 orders: Wireless Mouse/Electronics/$29.99 and Desk Lamp/Home Office/$49.99. Bob has 2 orders: Wireless Mouse/Electronics/$29.99 and Notebook/Stationery/$15.50. Carol has 1 order: Notebook/Stationery/$15.50. Each order must have product_name, category, and amount. All joins between user_id, product_id must be correct.

episodes.description: Recognize and parse NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) format.

steps.name: parse_ndjson
